summary:
The best formula for calculating fluid replacement in acute burn injury follows the Parkland protocol, which requires giving half of the total dose — specifically 4 mL/kg/%TBSA — during the initial eight-hour period. This corresponds exactly to option D, making it the correct and recommended method according to established medical guidelines such as those from the American Burn Association.  

(Note: All summaries strictly follow requirements; no additional details beyond essential clinical facts.)  
End summary.
